tax.summary: Canada taxes residents on worldwide income. US-Canada tax treaty coordinates taxation of US pensions and Social Security. Foreign Tax Credit typically offsets most double taxation. Provincial taxes add 5-25% on top of federal rates.
tax.key_exemptions: US-Canada tax treaty is one of the most comprehensive. Social Security benefits may be partially exempt. Foreign Tax Credit handles most double taxation. No special retirement visa but immigration pathways available.
tax.top_reason: English-speaking, safe, familiar culture

visa.name: Super Visa / Express Entry
visa.income_requirement_str: Super Visa: child/grandchild must meet income threshold
visa.summary: No dedicated retirement visa. Super Visa allows parents/grandparents of Canadian citizens to stay up to 5 years. Express Entry for those with qualifying skills or family. Popular for those with Canadian family connections.
visa.healthcare_notes: Excellent universal healthcare for legal residents. Provincial health coverage varies. Private insurance recommended while establishing residency. Medicare does not cover Canadian care.

expat.expat_vibe: Most natural transition from US. Familiar culture, English language, similar systems. Victoria BC particularly popular with US retirees for mild climate. Montreal bilingual and culturally distinct. Toronto world-class diverse city.

demographics.expat_worship_note: All denominations widely available. English-language worship universally available.

direct_us_cities: All major US cities — extensive cross-border routes

tax.summary: Progressive tax with 12% up to PLN 120,000 and 32% above. No special retirement regime. EU member. US-Poland tax treaty in place.
tax.key_exemptions: Lower bracket 12% applies to most average retirement incomes. Foreign Tax Credit available. No special exemptions for foreign pensions.
tax.top_reason: Very affordable EU country, rich history

visa.name: Temporary Residence Permit
visa.summary: Temporary residence for passive income earners. EU membership provides stability and Schengen travel. Poland is increasingly popular with expats.
visa.healthcare_notes: Public healthcare available to registered residents. Quality improving but variable. Private insurance recommended.

demographics.expat_worship_note: Catholic churches everywhere. International English services in Warsaw and Krakow.

tax.summary: Progressive income tax up to 27.5%. Foreign pensions may be taxable depending on residency status. Complex tax system. US-Brazil tax treaty limited. Consult tax professional.
tax.key_exemptions: Some foreign pension exemptions available. Foreign Tax Credit for US obligations. Tax rules complex and frequently change.
tax.top_reason: Diverse lifestyle options, beaches, vibrant culture

visa.name: Retirement Visa (VITEM XIV)
visa.summary: Retirement visa for those with provable pension income. Large country with diverse options from beaches to cities. Large expat communities in Florianopolis and Sao Paulo.
visa.healthcare_notes: Good private healthcare in major cities. SUS public system available but variable quality. Private insurance strongly recommended.

demographics.expat_worship_note: Catholic and Evangelical churches everywhere. English-language services in major cities.

tax.summary: Italy's Article 24-ter allows qualifying foreign pensioners to pay a flat 7% tax on all foreign income for up to 10 years. Must relocate to specific southern Italian municipalities. US-Italy tax treaty in place.
tax.key_exemptions: 7% flat tax on foreign pension income for up to 10 years. Must move to qualifying southern Italian town with population under 20,000. Significant savings vs standard progressive rates up to 43%.
tax.top_reason: 7% flat tax on foreign income for 10 years

visa.name: Elective Residency Visa
visa.summary: Elective Residency Visa for retirees with passive income. Requires proof of stable passive income and private health insurance. Leads to permanent residency after 5 years.
visa.healthcare_notes: Excellent public healthcare (SSN) accessible after registering as resident. Private insurance required for initial visa.

expat.expat_vibe: Strong American expat community especially in Florence and Rome. Many retirees drawn by 7% flat tax in southern villages. Italian bureaucracy notorious but expats navigate it. Warm culture once you're in.

demographics.expat_worship_note: Catholic churches everywhere. English-language Mass available in major cities. Protestant services in larger cities.

tax.key_exemptions: US-NZ tax treaty reduces double taxation. Foreign Tax Credit available. 4-year transitional resident exemption on foreign income for new residents.
tax.top_reason: English-speaking, safe, excellent quality of life

visa.name: Investor / Skilled Migrant (no dedicated retirement visa)
visa.summary: No dedicated retirement visa. Investor visa or skilled migrant pathways. Parent visa available. Popular for quality of life, safety, English language.
visa.healthcare_notes: Excellent public healthcare for residents. Private insurance recommended for faster access. High standard comparable to UK system.

expat.expat_vibe: Easy transition for Americans. English, friendly people, outdoor lifestyle. Smaller and quieter than Australia. Queenstown adventure capital of the world. Very high quality of life. Remote from rest of the world is the main complaint.

demographics.expat_worship_note: Christian churches of all denominations widely available. English-language worship universally available.

tax.key_exemptions: Low flat tax rate. Foreign Tax Credit reduces US liability. No special retirement visa but long-term residence available for passive income earners.
tax.top_reason: Low flat tax, affordable EU, Prague lifestyle

visa.name: Long-term Residence Permit
visa.summary: Long-term residence permit available for those with proof of passive income. Prague is a top expat destination. EU Schengen access.
visa.healthcare_notes: High quality public healthcare for registered residents. Private insurance recommended initially.

demographics.expat_worship_note: Catholic and Protestant churches available. International English services in Prague.

tax.summary: Serbia has a 15% flat personal income tax. Not EU member but candidate status. Low cost of living. Belgrade is a growing expat hub. No US-Serbia tax treaty.
tax.key_exemptions: 15% flat tax rate. No formal retirement visa but long-term residence available. Foreign Tax Credit available for US obligations.
tax.top_reason: Very affordable, growing expat scene in Belgrade

visa.name: Temporary Residence Permit
visa.summary: Temporary residence available for passive income earners. No formal retirement visa program. Low barriers to entry. EU candidate country.
visa.healthcare_notes: Public healthcare available but limited for foreigners. Private insurance recommended. Good private clinics in Belgrade.

demographics.expat_worship_note: Orthodox churches everywhere. Growing international community in Belgrade with some English services.

tax.rate_label: Pension income exempt from Philippine tax
tax.summary: SRRV holders with pension income are exempt from Philippine income tax on that pension. Territorial tax system for non-residents. English widely spoken. US has strong historical ties.
tax.key_exemptions: Pension income tax exempt for SRRV holders. No Philippine tax on US Social Security or foreign pensions. English as official language eliminates language barrier.
tax.top_reason: Pension income tax exempt, English-speaking, island lifestyle

visa.name: SRRV (Special Resident Retiree's Visa)
visa.summary: SRRV grants permanent residence. Age 50+ with pension: $1,500/month + $20,000 bank deposit. Without pension: $50,000 deposit. English-speaking, 7,000+ islands. Large US expat community.
visa.healthcare_notes: Good private hospitals in Manila and Cebu. English-speaking doctors. Private insurance recommended. Medicare not accepted abroad.

expat.expat_vibe: Largest English-speaking Asian retirement destination. American cultural influence very strong. Filipino people exceptionally warm toward Americans. SRRV program well established. Dumaguete called 'city of gentle people' popular with retirees. BGC Manila feels like US suburb.

demographics.expat_worship_note: Catholic churches everywhere. Wide variety of Protestant denominations. Very familiar Christian culture for Americans.

tax.summary: Bulgaria has the lowest flat income tax in the EU at 10%. Applies to all income including foreign pensions. EU member. Very low cost of living. US-Bulgaria tax treaty in place.
tax.key_exemptions: 10% flat tax on worldwide income for residents. Foreign Tax Credit easily covers US obligations. No complex brackets or phase-outs.
tax.top_reason: 10% flat tax, lowest cost EU country

visa.name: Long-term Residence Permit
visa.summary: Long-term residence available for passive income earners. Low income threshold. EU Schengen access. Black Sea coast and mountain regions popular.
visa.healthcare_notes: Public healthcare available but quality is inconsistent. Private insurance strongly recommended. Good private clinics in Sofia.

demographics.expat_worship_note: Orthodox churches everywhere. Limited English-language services.

tax.summary: Progressive income tax 14-45% plus 5.5% solidarity surcharge. Foreign pensions taxable for German residents. US-Germany tax treaty coordinates taxation. Foreign Tax Credit handles most double taxation. High quality of life but significant tax burden.
tax.key_exemptions: US-Germany tax treaty provides pension tax coordination. Foreign Tax Credit offsets US liability. No special retirement exemption. High cost but excellent infrastructure and healthcare.
tax.top_reason: World-class infrastructure, central Europe location

visa.name: Residence Permit for Financial Independence
visa.summary: Residence permit available for financially independent persons. Requires proof of sufficient income and health insurance. EU Schengen access. Berlin, Munich, Hamburg popular with expats.
visa.healthcare_notes: World-class public healthcare system. Health insurance mandatory for all residents. Private insurance available. Excellent standard of care. Medicare not accepted.

expat.expat_vibe: Berlin especially has massive international expat scene. Very liberal and creative city. English widely spoken. German efficiency makes practical life easy. Good public transport. Strong social services.

demographics.expat_worship_note: Catholic and Protestant churches everywhere. International English-language services in major cities.

tax.summary: Progressive income tax 20-45% plus National Insurance. US pensions taxable in UK as foreign pension income. UK state pension also taxable in US. US-UK tax treaty prevents double taxation via Foreign Tax Credit. Both countries tax pension income but credit offsets liability.
tax.key_exemptions: US-UK tax treaty (Article 17) governs pension taxation. Foreign Tax Credit on Form 1116 typically offsets most US liability. No special retirement exemption but treaty coordination reduces double tax.
tax.top_reason: English-speaking, culture, NHS healthcare

visa.name: Standard Visitor / Ancestry Visa
visa.summary: No dedicated retirement visa for non-EU nationals. UK Ancestry visa available if grandparent was UK citizen. Standard visitor visa allows 6-month stays. Long-term residency requires investment or family route.
visa.healthcare_notes: Excellent NHS public healthcare for legal residents. Private insurance recommended for faster access. World-class hospitals. Medicare does not cover UK care.

expat.expat_vibe: Most natural transition for Americans due to language and cultural similarities. London massive international city. Easy practical life. NHS healthcare highly regarded. No culture shock. Expensive but familiar.

demographics.expat_worship_note: All denominations available. English-language worship universally available.

direct_us_cities: All major US cities — most connected destination from US

visa.name: MVV Long Stay Visa
visa.summary: MVV long-stay visa for financially independent persons. Leads to residence permit. Amsterdam, Hague, Rotterdam are major expat hubs. English widely spoken.
visa.healthcare_notes: Excellent mandatory health insurance. All residents must have basic health insurance. High standard of care. Medicare not accepted.

tax.summary: Once resident in France, distributions from US retirement accounts (401k, IRA) are exempt from French local taxes. France preserves Roth account tax-exempt status. Progressive income tax otherwise up to 45%. US-France tax treaty in place.
tax.key_exemptions: US pension and Social Security income exempt from French tax under US-France treaty. Roth distributions maintain tax-exempt status. Foreign Tax Credit available for any remaining liability.
tax.top_reason: US pension and 401k distributions exempt from local tax

visa.name: Long Stay Visa / Talent Passport
visa.summary: Long Stay Visitor Visa for retirees with passive income. Must prove sufficient income to support yourself. Leads to residency card renewal annually, then 10-year card.
visa.healthcare_notes: World-class public healthcare (Securite Sociale) accessible after 3 months legal residency. Private insurance recommended initially.

expat.expat_vibe: One of the most popular destinations for American expats globally. Large established community. The American Church in Paris is a famous expat hub. French can be reserved but warm once relationships form. Bureaucracy challenging.

demographics.expat_worship_note: Catholic churches everywhere. American Church in Paris. Protestant and other services in major cities.

tax.summary: Progressive income tax up to 40%. Most stable and developed economy in South America. No special retirement exemptions. US-Chile tax treaty in place.
tax.key_exemptions: US-Chile tax treaty helps reduce double taxation. Foreign Tax Credit available. Santiago has first-world infrastructure.
tax.top_reason: Most stable South American country, world-class Santiago

visa.name: Rentista Visa
visa.summary: Rentista visa for passive income earners. Santiago is the most developed city in South America. Diverse geography from desert to Patagonia.
visa.healthcare_notes: Excellent private healthcare in Santiago at affordable prices. Private insurance recommended. Best healthcare system in South America.

demographics.expat_worship_note: Catholic churches everywhere. English-language services in Santiago expat areas.

tax.summary: Article 5B allows qualifying foreign pensioners to elect a 7% flat tax on all foreign source income for up to 15 years. Must come from a country with tax cooperation agreement with Greece. US qualifies.
tax.key_exemptions: 7% flat tax on all foreign income for up to 15 years. Requires minimum €250,000 real estate investment for Golden Visa OR €3,500/month income for retirement visa. Excellent tax deal for US retirees.
tax.top_reason: 7% flat tax on foreign income for 15 years

visa.name: Financially Independent Person Visa
visa.summary: Financially Independent Person Visa requires proof of passive income, health insurance, and clean criminal record. Golden Visa (real estate investment) also available. EU Schengen access.
visa.healthcare_notes: Public healthcare accessible after residency. Private insurance required for visa. High quality private hospitals in Athens and major cities.

expat.expat_vibe: Growing expat scene attracted by 7% flat tax program. Less established than Spain or Portugal but growing rapidly. Greek people warm and hospitable. Laid-back Mediterranean lifestyle.

demographics.expat_worship_note: Greek Orthodox churches everywhere. Catholic and Protestant churches in Athens and major tourist islands.

tax.summary: Progressive income tax 15-40%. No special retirement visa but long-term residence available. Istanbul popular with expats. High inflation environment in recent years.
tax.key_exemptions: No special pension exemptions. Foreign Tax Credit available. Dollar/euro purchasing power strong due to lira depreciation.
tax.top_reason: Affordable, Istanbul lifestyle, strong dollar value

demographics.expat_worship_note: Mosques everywhere. Catholic, Protestant, and Orthodox churches in Istanbul and major cities.

tax.summary: Canada taxes residents on worldwide income. US-Canada tax treaty coordinates taxation of US pensions and Social Security. Foreign Tax Credit typically offsets most double taxation. Provincial taxes add 5-25% on top of federal rates.
tax.key_exemptions: US-Canada tax treaty is one of the most comprehensive. Social Security benefits may be partially exempt. Foreign Tax Credit handles most double taxation. No special retirement visa but immigration pathways available.
tax.top_reason: English-speaking, safe, familiar culture

visa.name: Super Visa / Express Entry
visa.income_requirement_str: Super Visa: child/grandchild must meet income threshold
visa.summary: No dedicated retirement visa. Super Visa allows parents/grandparents of Canadian citizens to stay up to 5 years. Express Entry for those with qualifying skills or family. Popular for those with Canadian family connections.
visa.healthcare_notes: Excellent universal healthcare for legal residents. Provincial health coverage varies. Private insurance recommended while establishing residency. Medicare does not cover Canadian care.

expat.expat_vibe: Most natural transition from US. Familiar culture, English language, similar systems. Victoria BC particularly popular with US retirees for mild climate. Montreal bilingual and culturally distinct. Toronto world-class diverse city.

demographics.expat_worship_note: All denominations widely available. English-language worship universally available.

direct_us_cities: All major US cities — extensive cross-border routes

tax.summary: Italy's Article 24-ter allows qualifying foreign pensioners to pay a flat 7% tax on all foreign income for up to 10 years. Must relocate to specific southern Italian municipalities. US-Italy tax treaty in place.
tax.key_exemptions: 7% flat tax on foreign pension income for up to 10 years. Must move to qualifying southern Italian town with population under 20,000. Significant savings vs standard progressive rates up to 43%.
tax.top_reason: 7% flat tax on foreign income for 10 years

visa.name: Elective Residency Visa
visa.summary: Elective Residency Visa for retirees with passive income. Requires proof of stable passive income and private health insurance. Leads to permanent residency after 5 years.
visa.healthcare_notes: Excellent public healthcare (SSN) accessible after registering as resident. Private insurance required for initial visa.

expat.expat_vibe: Strong American expat community especially in Florence and Rome. Many retirees drawn by 7% flat tax in southern villages. Italian bureaucracy notorious but expats navigate it. Warm culture once you're in.

demographics.expat_worship_note: Catholic churches everywhere. English-language Mass available in major cities. Protestant services in larger cities.

tax.summary: Malaysia exempts foreign-source income from Malaysian tax for residents. US pensions and Social Security not taxable locally. Territorial tax system. MM2H program restructured into tiered Silver/Gold/Platinum levels.
tax.key_exemptions: All foreign-source income completely exempt from Malaysian tax. Only Malaysian-source income taxable. English widely spoken. High quality of life.
tax.top_reason: Foreign income exempt, world-class healthcare, English spoken

visa.name: MM2H (Malaysia My Second Home)
visa.summary: MM2H program now has Silver ($2,500/month), Gold ($5,000/month), and Platinum tiers. Kuala Lumpur and Penang are top expat cities. English widely spoken. Excellent healthcare.
visa.healthcare_notes: World-class private healthcare in KL and Penang. International hospitals with English-speaking staff. Private insurance recommended but affordable.

expat.expat_vibe: Very expat-friendly. English is widely spoken making it easiest Asian country for English-speaking expats. MM2H program designed specifically for long-term residents. Multi-cultural society means expats blend in easily. Penang especially popular for retirees.

demographics.expat_worship_note: Mosques, Buddhist temples, churches, and Hindu temples all present. English-language churches widely available.

tax.summary: Romania has one of the lowest flat income tax rates in the EU at 10%. No special retirement regime but very low base rate. EU and Schengen member as of 2025. US-Romania tax treaty in place.
tax.key_exemptions: 10% flat tax on all income including pensions. Very favorable for US retirees as Foreign Tax Credit easily offsets US obligations. Bucharest emerging as expat hub.
tax.top_reason: 10% flat tax, very affordable EU country

visa.name: Long-term Residence Permit
visa.summary: Residence permit available for passive income earners. Low income requirements. EU and Schengen access as of 2025.
visa.healthcare_notes: Public healthcare available but quality is variable. Private insurance strongly recommended. Good private hospitals in Bucharest and major cities.

demographics.expat_worship_note: Orthodox churches everywhere. Catholic and Protestant services available. Limited English services.

tax.summary: Once resident in France, distributions from US retirement accounts (401k, IRA) are exempt from French local taxes. France preserves Roth account tax-exempt status. Progressive income tax otherwise up to 45%. US-France tax treaty in place.
tax.key_exemptions: US pension and Social Security income exempt from French tax under US-France treaty. Roth distributions maintain tax-exempt status. Foreign Tax Credit available for any remaining liability.
tax.top_reason: US pension and 401k distributions exempt from local tax

visa.name: Long Stay Visa / Talent Passport
visa.summary: Long Stay Visitor Visa for retirees with passive income. Must prove sufficient income to support yourself. Leads to residency card renewal annually, then 10-year card.
visa.healthcare_notes: World-class public healthcare (Securite Sociale) accessible after 3 months legal residency. Private insurance recommended initially.

expat.expat_vibe: One of the most popular destinations for American expats globally. Large established community. The American Church in Paris is a famous expat hub. French can be reserved but warm once relationships form. Bureaucracy challenging.

demographics.expat_worship_note: Catholic churches everywhere. American Church in Paris. Protestant and other services in major cities.

tax.summary: Progressive income tax 14-45% plus 5.5% solidarity surcharge. Foreign pensions taxable for German residents. US-Germany tax treaty coordinates taxation. Foreign Tax Credit handles most double taxation. High quality of life but significant tax burden.
tax.key_exemptions: US-Germany tax treaty provides pension tax coordination. Foreign Tax Credit offsets US liability. No special retirement exemption. High cost but excellent infrastructure and healthcare.
tax.top_reason: World-class infrastructure, central Europe location

visa.name: Residence Permit for Financial Independence
visa.summary: Residence permit available for financially independent persons. Requires proof of sufficient income and health insurance. EU Schengen access. Berlin, Munich, Hamburg popular with expats.
visa.healthcare_notes: World-class public healthcare system. Health insurance mandatory for all residents. Private insurance available. Excellent standard of care. Medicare not accepted.

expat.expat_vibe: Berlin especially has massive international expat scene. Very liberal and creative city. English widely spoken. German efficiency makes practical life easy. Good public transport. Strong social services.

demographics.expat_worship_note: Catholic and Protestant churches everywhere. International English-language services in major cities.

tax.rate_label: Non-resident: no local tax on foreign income
tax.summary: If income is predominantly from US sources and you maintain a home outside Mexico, you may qualify as a non-resident, meaning US pension income is not taxed in Mexico. Full tax residency (183+ days) subjects worldwide income to Mexican taxes up to 35%.
tax.key_exemptions: Non-resident status means US pension income not taxed locally. Stay under 183 days or maintain non-resident status to preserve this. US-Mexico tax treaty in place.
tax.top_reason: Close to US, large expat communities, no local tax on foreign income

visa.name: Temporary Resident Visa
visa.summary: Temporary Resident Visa requires ~$2,700/month income or $108,000 in savings. Leads to permanent residency after 4 years. Large expat communities in San Miguel, Puerto Vallarta, Merida.
visa.healthcare_notes: Excellent private healthcare at fraction of US cost. IMSS public system available. Private insurance recommended. Many US expats use Mexican private care.

expat.expat_vibe: Largest American expat community in the world. Highly established infrastructure for expats. Easy transition for Americans. Some areas feel like American enclaves. Lake Chapala/Ajijic specifically known as top retirement community.

demographics.expat_worship_note: Catholic churches everywhere. English-language Protestant and non-denominational churches in major expat areas.

direct_us_cities: All major US cities — extensive direct routes

tax.summary: NHR regime closed to new applicants in 2024. Foreign pensions now taxed at standard progressive rates up to 48%. US-Portugal tax treaty helps reduce double taxation. Foreign Tax Credit usually offsets most US tax liability.
tax.key_exemptions: NHR 2.0 (IFICI) replaced NHR but excludes pension income. Existing NHR holders grandfathered through their 10-year period. US-Portugal tax treaty provides some relief.
tax.top_reason: Affordable Europe, English widely spoken

visa.name: D7 Passive Income Visa
visa.summary: D7 visa for passive income earners including retirees. Leads to permanent residency after 5 years, citizenship after 5 years. Requires proof of passive income and private health insurance.
visa.healthcare_notes: Public SNS healthcare accessible after residency validated. Private insurance required for visa application. High quality public system once enrolled.

expat.expat_vibe: One of the most welcoming expat destinations in Europe. Large English-speaking community. Many digital nomads and retirees. Portuguese people generally warm toward Americans.

demographics.expat_worship_note: Catholic churches everywhere. Protestant and international English-language services in Lisbon and Porto.

tax.summary: Progressive tax with 12% up to PLN 120,000 and 32% above. No special retirement regime. EU member. US-Poland tax treaty in place.
tax.key_exemptions: Lower bracket 12% applies to most average retirement incomes. Foreign Tax Credit available. No special exemptions for foreign pensions.
tax.top_reason: Very affordable EU country, rich history

visa.name: Temporary Residence Permit
visa.summary: Temporary residence for passive income earners. EU membership provides stability and Schengen travel. Poland is increasingly popular with expats.
visa.healthcare_notes: Public healthcare available to registered residents. Quality improving but variable. Private insurance recommended.

demographics.expat_worship_note: Catholic churches everywhere. International English services in Warsaw and Krakow.

tax.summary: No special tax regime for pension income. Foreign pensions taxed at standard progressive rates. Spain's Golden Visa closed April 2025. Beckham Law excludes passive income. US-Spain tax treaty in place.
tax.key_exemptions: Non-Lucrative Visa requires ~€28,800/year passive income. No flat-rate pension exemption available. Foreign Tax Credit offsets most US tax owed.
tax.top_reason: Mediterranean lifestyle, warm climate

visa.name: Non-Lucrative Visa (NLV)
visa.summary: Non-Lucrative Visa for retirees with passive income. Golden Visa closed April 2025. Leads to permanent residency after 5 years. Annual renewal required initially.
visa.healthcare_notes: Excellent public healthcare once registered as resident. Private insurance required for initial visa application.

expat.expat_vibe: Massive established expat community especially British. American community growing rapidly. Vibrant social scene. Easy to meet other expats. Spanish people sociable but expats can stay in expat bubbles.

demographics.expat_worship_note: Catholic churches everywhere. International English churches in major cities and expat areas.

tax.summary: Progressive income tax 20-45% plus National Insurance. US pensions taxable in UK as foreign pension income. UK state pension also taxable in US. US-UK tax treaty prevents double taxation via Foreign Tax Credit. Both countries tax pension income but credit offsets liability.
tax.key_exemptions: US-UK tax treaty (Article 17) governs pension taxation. Foreign Tax Credit on Form 1116 typically offsets most US liability. No special retirement exemption but treaty coordination reduces double tax.
tax.top_reason: English-speaking, culture, NHS healthcare

visa.name: Standard Visitor / Ancestry Visa
visa.summary: No dedicated retirement visa for non-EU nationals. UK Ancestry visa available if grandparent was UK citizen. Standard visitor visa allows 6-month stays. Long-term residency requires investment or family route.
visa.healthcare_notes: Excellent NHS public healthcare for legal residents. Private insurance recommended for faster access. World-class hospitals. Medicare does not cover UK care.

expat.expat_vibe: Most natural transition for Americans due to language and cultural similarities. London massive international city. Easy practical life. NHS healthcare highly regarded. No culture shock. Expensive but familiar.

demographics.expat_worship_note: All denominations available. English-language worship universally available.

visa.name: Long-term Residence Permit
visa.summary: Long-term residence permit available for those with proof of passive income. Prague is a top expat destination. EU Schengen access.
visa.healthcare_notes: High quality public healthcare for registered residents. Private insurance recommended initially.

demographics.expat_worship_note: Catholic and Protestant churches available. International English services in Prague.

tax.summary: Progressive income tax 10-50%. New immigrants and returning residents receive 10-year tax exemption on foreign income. US-Israel treaty in place. Unique immigration law: Law of Return for Jewish immigrants.
tax.key_exemptions: 10-year foreign income tax exemption for new immigrants (Olim) and returning residents. US-Israel treaty reduces double taxation. Law of Return provides citizenship path for eligible Jewish immigrants.
tax.top_reason: 10-year foreign income exemption, Law of Return, world-class healthcare

visa.name: Aliyah (Law of Return) or temporary residency
visa.summary: Law of Return grants citizenship and residency rights to eligible Jewish immigrants and family members. Temporary residency available for others. Tel Aviv and Jerusalem major expat hubs.
visa.healthcare_notes: Excellent universal healthcare via health funds (kupot holim). Very high standard. English widely spoken in medical settings. Medicare not accepted.

tax.summary: Romania has one of the lowest flat income tax rates in the EU at 10%. No special retirement regime but very low base rate. EU and Schengen member as of 2025. US-Romania tax treaty in place.
tax.key_exemptions: 10% flat tax on all income including pensions. Very favorable for US retirees as Foreign Tax Credit easily offsets US obligations. Bucharest emerging as expat hub.
tax.top_reason: 10% flat tax, very affordable EU country

visa.name: Long-term Residence Permit
visa.summary: Residence permit available for passive income earners. Low income requirements. EU and Schengen access as of 2025.
visa.healthcare_notes: Public healthcare available but quality is variable. Private insurance strongly recommended. Good private hospitals in Bucharest and major cities.

demographics.expat_worship_note: Orthodox churches everywhere. Catholic and Protestant services available. Limited English services.

tax.summary: Progressive income tax 14-45% plus 5.5% solidarity surcharge. Foreign pensions taxable for German residents. US-Germany tax treaty coordinates taxation. Foreign Tax Credit handles most double taxation. High quality of life but significant tax burden.
tax.key_exemptions: US-Germany tax treaty provides pension tax coordination. Foreign Tax Credit offsets US liability. No special retirement exemption. High cost but excellent infrastructure and healthcare.
tax.top_reason: World-class infrastructure, central Europe location

visa.name: Residence Permit for Financial Independence
visa.summary: Residence permit available for financially independent persons. Requires proof of sufficient income and health insurance. EU Schengen access. Berlin, Munich, Hamburg popular with expats.
visa.healthcare_notes: World-class public healthcare system. Health insurance mandatory for all residents. Private insurance available. Excellent standard of care. Medicare not accepted.

expat.expat_vibe: Berlin especially has massive international expat scene. Very liberal and creative city. English widely spoken. German efficiency makes practical life easy. Good public transport. Strong social services.

demographics.expat_worship_note: Catholic and Protestant churches everywhere. International English-language services in major cities.

tax.summary: Progressive tax with 12% up to PLN 120,000 and 32% above. No special retirement regime. EU member. US-Poland tax treaty in place.
tax.key_exemptions: Lower bracket 12% applies to most average retirement incomes. Foreign Tax Credit available. No special exemptions for foreign pensions.
tax.top_reason: Very affordable EU country, rich history

visa.name: Temporary Residence Permit
visa.summary: Temporary residence for passive income earners. EU membership provides stability and Schengen travel. Poland is increasingly popular with expats.
visa.healthcare_notes: Public healthcare available to registered residents. Quality improving but variable. Private insurance recommended.

demographics.expat_worship_note: Catholic churches everywhere. International English services in Warsaw and Krakow.

tax.summary: Progressive income tax 20-45% plus National Insurance. US pensions taxable in UK as foreign pension income. UK state pension also taxable in US. US-UK tax treaty prevents double taxation via Foreign Tax Credit. Both countries tax pension income but credit offsets liability.
tax.key_exemptions: US-UK tax treaty (Article 17) governs pension taxation. Foreign Tax Credit on Form 1116 typically offsets most US liability. No special retirement exemption but treaty coordination reduces double tax.
tax.top_reason: English-speaking, culture, NHS healthcare

visa.name: Standard Visitor / Ancestry Visa
visa.summary: No dedicated retirement visa for non-EU nationals. UK Ancestry visa available if grandparent was UK citizen. Standard visitor visa allows 6-month stays. Long-term residency requires investment or family route.
visa.healthcare_notes: Excellent NHS public healthcare for legal residents. Private insurance recommended for faster access. World-class hospitals. Medicare does not cover UK care.

expat.expat_vibe: Most natural transition for Americans due to language and cultural similarities. London massive international city. Easy practical life. NHS healthcare highly regarded. No culture shock. Expensive but familiar.

demographics.expat_worship_note: All denominations available. English-language worship universally available.

direct_us_cities: All major US cities — most connected destination from US

tax.summary: Canada taxes residents on worldwide income. US-Canada tax treaty coordinates taxation of US pensions and Social Security. Foreign Tax Credit typically offsets most double taxation. Provincial taxes add 5-25% on top of federal rates.
tax.key_exemptions: US-Canada tax treaty is one of the most comprehensive. Social Security benefits may be partially exempt. Foreign Tax Credit handles most double taxation. No special retirement visa but immigration pathways available.
tax.top_reason: English-speaking, safe, familiar culture

visa.name: Super Visa / Express Entry
visa.income_requirement_str: Super Visa: child/grandchild must meet income threshold
visa.summary: No dedicated retirement visa. Super Visa allows parents/grandparents of Canadian citizens to stay up to 5 years. Express Entry for those with qualifying skills or family. Popular for those with Canadian family connections.
visa.healthcare_notes: Excellent universal healthcare for legal residents. Provincial health coverage varies. Private insurance recommended while establishing residency. Medicare does not cover Canadian care.

expat.expat_vibe: Most natural transition from US. Familiar culture, English language, similar systems. Victoria BC particularly popular with US retirees for mild climate. Montreal bilingual and culturally distinct. Toronto world-class diverse city.

demographics.expat_worship_note: All denominations widely available. English-language worship universally available.

direct_us_cities: All major US cities — extensive cross-border routes

tax.key_exemptions: US-Sweden treaty reduces double taxation. Foreign Tax Credit available. No special retirement exemption. Strong social safety net.
tax.top_reason: Safety, social services, Scandinavian lifestyle

visa.name: Residence Permit
visa.summary: Residence permit for financially independent persons. Stockholm popular with expats. High quality of life, safety, and social services.
visa.healthcare_notes: Excellent universal healthcare for residents. Very high standard of care. Medicare not accepted.

tax.summary: Progressive income tax up to 51.4%. High tax burden but excellent services. US-Finland treaty in place. Helsinki has large expat community. World's happiest country rankings.
tax.key_exemptions: US-Finland treaty reduces double taxation. Foreign Tax Credit available. No special retirement exemption.
tax.top_reason: Safety, happiness rankings, clean environment

visa.name: Residence Permit
visa.summary: Residence permit for financially independent persons. Helsinki and coastal areas popular. Excellent public services and safety.
visa.healthcare_notes: Excellent universal healthcare for residents. High standard of care. Medicare not accepted.

tax.summary: Progressive income tax up to 50% plus communal taxes. High tax burden. US-Belgium treaty in place. Foreign Tax Credit available. Brussels is EU capital with large international community.
tax.key_exemptions: US-Belgium treaty reduces double taxation. Foreign Tax Credit offsets liability. No special retirement exemption. Brussels has excellent international infrastructure.
tax.top_reason: Brussels expat community, EU capital, central location

visa.name: Long-Stay Visa / Residence Permit
visa.summary: Long-stay visa for financially independent persons. Brussels has large expat and EU institution community. Excellent healthcare and public services.
visa.healthcare_notes: Excellent public healthcare for registered residents. Health insurance required. High standard of care. Medicare not accepted.

tax.summary: Australia taxes residents on worldwide income at progressive rates up to 45% plus a 2% Medicare levy. US-Australia tax treaty in place but contains savings clause limiting benefits for US citizens. Foreign Tax Credit typically offsets most US tax liability. No special retirement tax regime.
tax.key_exemptions: US-Australia tax treaty helps reduce double taxation but does not eliminate US filing obligations. Foreign Tax Credit on Form 1116 is the primary relief tool. Temporary residents taxed only on Australian-source income. No special pension exemptions for US retirees.
tax.top_reason: English-speaking, high quality of life, excellent healthcare

visa.name: Subclass 405 / 410 (effectively closed) or Contributory Parent
visa.income_requirement_str: No standard retirement visa currently available
visa.summary: Australia's dedicated retirement visas (subclass 405 and 410) are effectively closed to new applicants. Most US retirees use partner visas, parent visas, or investor pathways. No straightforward retirement visa route currently exists. Consult an Australian immigration specialist.
visa.healthcare_notes: Excellent public Medicare system accessible to permanent residents. Private health insurance recommended and often required for visa. World-class hospitals in Sydney, Melbourne, and major cities. Medicare does not cover care in Australia.

expat.expat_vibe: Very easy transition for Americans. English, similar culture, outdoor lifestyle. Sydney and Melbourne world-class cities. High quality of life. Expensive but wages are also high. Limited retirement visa pathway is the main challenge.

demographics.expat_worship_note: Christian churches of all denominations widely available. English-language worship universally available.

tax.summary: Once resident in France, distributions from US retirement accounts (401k, IRA) are exempt from French local taxes. France preserves Roth account tax-exempt status. Progressive income tax otherwise up to 45%. US-France tax treaty in place.
tax.key_exemptions: US pension and Social Security income exempt from French tax under US-France treaty. Roth distributions maintain tax-exempt status. Foreign Tax Credit available for any remaining liability.
tax.top_reason: US pension and 401k distributions exempt from local tax

visa.name: Long Stay Visa / Talent Passport
visa.summary: Long Stay Visitor Visa for retirees with passive income. Must prove sufficient income to support yourself. Leads to residency card renewal annually, then 10-year card.
visa.healthcare_notes: World-class public healthcare (Securite Sociale) accessible after 3 months legal residency. Private insurance recommended initially.

expat.expat_vibe: One of the most popular destinations for American expats globally. Large established community. The American Church in Paris is a famous expat hub. French can be reserved but warm once relationships form. Bureaucracy challenging.

demographics.expat_worship_note: Catholic churches everywhere. American Church in Paris. Protestant and other services in major cities.

tax.summary: Italy's Article 24-ter allows qualifying foreign pensioners to pay a flat 7% tax on all foreign income for up to 10 years. Must relocate to specific southern Italian municipalities. US-Italy tax treaty in place.
tax.key_exemptions: 7% flat tax on foreign pension income for up to 10 years. Must move to qualifying southern Italian town with population under 20,000. Significant savings vs standard progressive rates up to 43%.
tax.top_reason: 7% flat tax on foreign income for 10 years

visa.name: Elective Residency Visa
visa.summary: Elective Residency Visa for retirees with passive income. Requires proof of stable passive income and private health insurance. Leads to permanent residency after 5 years.
visa.healthcare_notes: Excellent public healthcare (SSN) accessible after registering as resident. Private insurance required for initial visa.

expat.expat_vibe: Strong American expat community especially in Florence and Rome. Many retirees drawn by 7% flat tax in southern villages. Italian bureaucracy notorious but expats navigate it. Warm culture once you're in.

demographics.expat_worship_note: Catholic churches everywhere. English-language Mass available in major cities. Protestant services in larger cities.

tax.rate_label: Non-resident: no local tax on foreign income
tax.summary: If income is predominantly from US sources and you maintain a home outside Mexico, you may qualify as a non-resident, meaning US pension income is not taxed in Mexico. Full tax residency (183+ days) subjects worldwide income to Mexican taxes up to 35%.
tax.key_exemptions: Non-resident status means US pension income not taxed locally. Stay under 183 days or maintain non-resident status to preserve this. US-Mexico tax treaty in place.
tax.top_reason: Close to US, large expat communities, no local tax on foreign income

visa.name: Temporary Resident Visa
visa.summary: Temporary Resident Visa requires ~$2,700/month income or $108,000 in savings. Leads to permanent residency after 4 years. Large expat communities in San Miguel, Puerto Vallarta, Merida.
visa.healthcare_notes: Excellent private healthcare at fraction of US cost. IMSS public system available. Private insurance recommended. Many US expats use Mexican private care.

expat.expat_vibe: Largest American expat community in the world. Highly established infrastructure for expats. Easy transition for Americans. Some areas feel like American enclaves. Lake Chapala/Ajijic specifically known as top retirement community.

demographics.expat_worship_note: Catholic churches everywhere. English-language Protestant and non-denominational churches in major expat areas.

direct_us_cities: All major US cities — extensive direct routes

visa.summary: No dedicated retirement visa. Super Visa allows parents/grandparents of Canadian citizens to stay up to 5 years. Express Entry for those with qualifying skills or family. Popular for those with Canadian family connections.
visa.healthcare_notes: Excellent universal healthcare for legal residents. Provincial health coverage varies. Private insurance recommended while establishing residency. Medicare does not cover Canadian care.

expat.expat_vibe: Most natural transition from US. Familiar culture, English language, similar systems. Victoria BC particularly popular with US retirees for mild climate. Montreal bilingual and culturally distinct. Toronto world-class diverse city.

demographics.expat_worship_note: All denominations widely available. English-language worship universally available.

direct_us_cities: All major US cities — extensive cross-border routes

tax.summary: NHR regime closed to new applicants in 2024. Foreign pensions now taxed at standard progressive rates up to 48%. US-Portugal tax treaty helps reduce double taxation. Foreign Tax Credit usually offsets most US tax liability.
tax.key_exemptions: NHR 2.0 (IFICI) replaced NHR but excludes pension income. Existing NHR holders grandfathered through their 10-year period. US-Portugal tax treaty provides some relief.
tax.top_reason: Affordable Europe, English widely spoken

visa.name: D7 Passive Income Visa
visa.summary: D7 visa for passive income earners including retirees. Leads to permanent residency after 5 years, citizenship after 5 years. Requires proof of passive income and private health insurance.
visa.healthcare_notes: Public SNS healthcare accessible after residency validated. Private insurance required for visa application. High quality public system once enrolled.

expat.expat_vibe: One of the most welcoming expat destinations in Europe. Large English-speaking community. Many digital nomads and retirees. Portuguese people generally warm toward Americans.

demographics.expat_worship_note: Catholic churches everywhere. Protestant and international English-language services in Lisbon and Porto.

tax.summary: Progressive income tax 10-50%. New immigrants and returning residents receive 10-year tax exemption on foreign income. US-Israel treaty in place. Unique immigration law: Law of Return for Jewish immigrants.
tax.key_exemptions: 10-year foreign income tax exemption for new immigrants (Olim) and returning residents. US-Israel treaty reduces double taxation. Law of Return provides citizenship path for eligible Jewish immigrants.
tax.top_reason: 10-year foreign income exemption, Law of Return, world-class healthcare

visa.name: Aliyah (Law of Return) or temporary residency
visa.summary: Law of Return grants citizenship and residency rights to eligible Jewish immigrants and family members. Temporary residency available for others. Tel Aviv and Jerusalem major expat hubs.
visa.healthcare_notes: Excellent universal healthcare via health funds (kupot holim). Very high standard. English widely spoken in medical settings. Medicare not accepted.

tax.rate_label: 5% flat tax on foreign pensions over €3,420
tax.summary: Cyprus offers a 5% flat tax on foreign pension income above €3,420 per year. Alternatively, taxed at standard rates up to 35%. Retirees choose the more favorable option. EU member.
tax.key_exemptions: 5% flat tax option on foreign pensions is one of the best rates in the EU. Territorial aspects for non-domiciled residents. US-Cyprus tax treaty in place.
tax.top_reason: 5% flat tax on foreign pensions, EU membership, sunny climate

visa.name: Category F Visa (Retired Persons)
visa.summary: Category F visa for retirees with passive income. Requires proof of income and health insurance. EU member with Schengen access. Popular with expats for weather and lifestyle.
visa.healthcare_notes: Good public and private healthcare. English widely spoken. Private insurance recommended. EU standard of care.

expat.community_size: Very Large relative to island size
expat.expat_vibe: Major British expat destination. English spoken everywhere due to British colonial history. Large Russian community also present. Paphos especially popular with retirees. Mediterranean lifestyle, good weather, EU membership.

demographics.expat_worship_note: Orthodox churches everywhere. English-language services available due to large British community.

tax.summary: One of the highest income tax rates in the world up to 55.9%. Foreign pensions taxable for residents. US-Denmark treaty in place. Exceptional quality of life, healthcare, and social services offset high taxes.
tax.key_exemptions: US-Denmark treaty reduces double taxation. Foreign Tax Credit available. No special retirement exemption. High happiness index and quality of life.
tax.top_reason: World's happiest country, exceptional quality of life

visa.name: Residence Permit
visa.summary: Residence permit for financially independent persons. Copenhagen is consistently top-ranked for quality of life. Very high standard of living.
visa.healthcare_notes: World-class universal healthcare. Free for residents. Very high standard. Medicare not accepted.

tax.summary: Once resident in France, distributions from US retirement accounts (401k, IRA) are exempt from French local taxes. France preserves Roth account tax-exempt status. Progressive income tax otherwise up to 45%. US-France tax treaty in place.
tax.key_exemptions: US pension and Social Security income exempt from French tax under US-France treaty. Roth distributions maintain tax-exempt status. Foreign Tax Credit available for any remaining liability.
tax.top_reason: US pension and 401k distributions exempt from local tax

visa.name: Long Stay Visa / Talent Passport
visa.summary: Long Stay Visitor Visa for retirees with passive income. Must prove sufficient income to support yourself. Leads to residency card renewal annually, then 10-year card.
visa.healthcare_notes: World-class public healthcare (Securite Sociale) accessible after 3 months legal residency. Private insurance recommended initially.

expat.expat_vibe: One of the most popular destinations for American expats globally. Large established community. The American Church in Paris is a famous expat hub. French can be reserved but warm once relationships form. Bureaucracy challenging.

demographics.expat_worship_note: Catholic churches everywhere. American Church in Paris. Protestant and other services in major cities.

tax.summary: Progressive income tax 19-25%. EU and Schengen member. Low cost of living for EU country. Bratislava close to Vienna and Budapest. US-Slovakia treaty in place.
tax.key_exemptions: US-Slovakia treaty reduces double taxation. Foreign Tax Credit available. Low rates for EU. Bratislava has growing expat scene.
tax.top_reason: Affordable EU, close to Vienna and Budapest

visa.name: Temporary Residence Permit
visa.summary: Temporary residence for financially independent persons. EU Schengen access. Bratislava very close to Vienna and Budapest.
visa.healthcare_notes: Public healthcare for registered residents. Private insurance recommended. EU standard. Medicare not accepted.

tax.summary: No special tax regime for pension income. Foreign pensions taxed at standard progressive rates. Spain's Golden Visa closed April 2025. Beckham Law excludes passive income. US-Spain tax treaty in place.
tax.key_exemptions: Non-Lucrative Visa requires ~€28,800/year passive income. No flat-rate pension exemption available. Foreign Tax Credit offsets most US tax owed.
tax.top_reason: Mediterranean lifestyle, warm climate

visa.name: Non-Lucrative Visa (NLV)
visa.summary: Non-Lucrative Visa for retirees with passive income. Golden Visa closed April 2025. Leads to permanent residency after 5 years. Annual renewal required initially.
visa.healthcare_notes: Excellent public healthcare once registered as resident. Private insurance required for initial visa application.

expat.expat_vibe: Massive established expat community especially British. American community growing rapidly. Vibrant social scene. Easy to meet other expats. Spanish people sociable but expats can stay in expat bubbles.

demographics.expat_worship_note: Catholic churches everywhere. International English churches in major cities and expat areas.

tax.summary: Progressive income tax up to 49.5%. Foreign pensions taxable for residents. US-Netherlands treaty in place. 30% ruling available for some expats (not applicable to retirees). High tax but excellent quality of life.
tax.key_exemptions: US-Netherlands treaty reduces double taxation. Foreign Tax Credit available. No special retirement exemption. Amsterdam and Hague popular with international community.
tax.top_reason: English-speaking, liberal culture, central Europe

visa.name: MVV Long Stay Visa
visa.summary: MVV long-stay visa for financially independent persons. Leads to residence permit. Amsterdam, Hague, Rotterdam are major expat hubs. English widely spoken.
visa.healthcare_notes: Excellent mandatory health insurance. All residents must have basic health insurance. High standard of care. Medicare not accepted.

tax.summary: Canada taxes residents on worldwide income. US-Canada tax treaty coordinates taxation of US pensions and Social Security. Foreign Tax Credit typically offsets most double taxation. Provincial taxes add 5-25% on top of federal rates.
tax.key_exemptions: US-Canada tax treaty is one of the most comprehensive. Social Security benefits may be partially exempt. Foreign Tax Credit handles most double taxation. No special retirement visa but immigration pathways available.
tax.top_reason: English-speaking, safe, familiar culture

visa.name: Super Visa / Express Entry
visa.income_requirement_str: Super Visa: child/grandchild must meet income threshold
visa.summary: No dedicated retirement visa. Super Visa allows parents/grandparents of Canadian citizens to stay up to 5 years. Express Entry for those with qualifying skills or family. Popular for those with Canadian family connections.
visa.healthcare_notes: Excellent universal healthcare for legal residents. Provincial health coverage varies. Private insurance recommended while establishing residency. Medicare does not cover Canadian care.

expat.expat_vibe: Most natural transition from US. Familiar culture, English language, similar systems. Victoria BC particularly popular with US retirees for mild climate. Montreal bilingual and culturally distinct. Toronto world-class diverse city.

demographics.expat_worship_note: All denominations widely available. English-language worship universally available.

direct_us_cities: All major US cities — extensive cross-border routes

tax.summary: Progressive income tax 14-45% plus 5.5% solidarity surcharge. Foreign pensions taxable for German residents. US-Germany tax treaty coordinates taxation. Foreign Tax Credit handles most double taxation. High quality of life but significant tax burden.
tax.key_exemptions: US-Germany tax treaty provides pension tax coordination. Foreign Tax Credit offsets US liability. No special retirement exemption. High cost but excellent infrastructure and healthcare.
tax.top_reason: World-class infrastructure, central Europe location

visa.name: Residence Permit for Financial Independence
visa.summary: Residence permit available for financially independent persons. Requires proof of sufficient income and health insurance. EU Schengen access. Berlin, Munich, Hamburg popular with expats.
visa.healthcare_notes: World-class public healthcare system. Health insurance mandatory for all residents. Private insurance available. Excellent standard of care. Medicare not accepted.

expat.expat_vibe: Berlin especially has massive international expat scene. Very liberal and creative city. English widely spoken. German efficiency makes practical life easy. Good public transport. Strong social services.

demographics.expat_worship_note: Catholic and Protestant churches everywhere. International English-language services in major cities.

tax.rate_label: Non-resident: no local tax on foreign income
tax.summary: If income is predominantly from US sources and you maintain a home outside Mexico, you may qualify as a non-resident, meaning US pension income is not taxed in Mexico. Full tax residency (183+ days) subjects worldwide income to Mexican taxes up to 35%.
tax.key_exemptions: Non-resident status means US pension income not taxed locally. Stay under 183 days or maintain non-resident status to preserve this. US-Mexico tax treaty in place.
tax.top_reason: Close to US, large expat communities, no local tax on foreign income

visa.name: Temporary Resident Visa
visa.summary: Temporary Resident Visa requires ~$2,700/month income or $108,000 in savings. Leads to permanent residency after 4 years. Large expat communities in San Miguel, Puerto Vallarta, Merida.
visa.healthcare_notes: Excellent private healthcare at fraction of US cost. IMSS public system available. Private insurance recommended. Many US expats use Mexican private care.

expat.expat_vibe: Largest American expat community in the world. Highly established infrastructure for expats. Easy transition for Americans. Some areas feel like American enclaves. Lake Chapala/Ajijic specifically known as top retirement community.

demographics.expat_worship_note: Catholic churches everywhere. English-language Protestant and non-denominational churches in major expat areas.

direct_us_cities: All major US cities — extensive direct routes

tax.summary: Progressive income tax up to 51.4%. High tax burden but excellent services. US-Finland treaty in place. Helsinki has large expat community. World's happiest country rankings.
tax.key_exemptions: US-Finland treaty reduces double taxation. Foreign Tax Credit available. No special retirement exemption.
tax.top_reason: Safety, happiness rankings, clean environment

visa.name: Residence Permit
visa.summary: Residence permit for financially independent persons. Helsinki and coastal areas popular. Excellent public services and safety.
visa.healthcare_notes: Excellent universal healthcare for residents. High standard of care. Medicare not accepted.

tax.summary: Progressive income tax 10-50%. New immigrants and returning residents receive 10-year tax exemption on foreign income. US-Israel treaty in place. Unique immigration law: Law of Return for Jewish immigrants.
tax.key_exemptions: 10-year foreign income tax exemption for new immigrants (Olim) and returning residents. US-Israel treaty reduces double taxation. Law of Return provides citizenship path for eligible Jewish immigrants.
tax.top_reason: 10-year foreign income exemption, Law of Return, world-class healthcare

visa.name: Aliyah (Law of Return) or temporary residency
visa.summary: Law of Return grants citizenship and residency rights to eligible Jewish immigrants and family members. Temporary residency available for others. Tel Aviv and Jerusalem major expat hubs.
visa.healthcare_notes: Excellent universal healthcare via health funds (kupot holim). Very high standard. English widely spoken in medical settings. Medicare not accepted.

tax.summary: Progressive income tax up to 45%. Foreign pension income taxable for residents. South Africa has a worldwide income tax system. Strong dollar purchasing power. US-South Africa tax treaty in place.
tax.key_exemptions: Foreign Tax Credit available. Strong dollar purchasing power. High quality of life in Cape Town and Johannesburg for those with dollar income.
tax.top_reason: Cape Town lifestyle, world-class private healthcare

visa.name: Retired Person Visa
visa.summary: Retired Person Visa for those with proven pension or retirement income. Cape Town consistently ranked top global lifestyle destination. Strong expat infrastructure.
visa.healthcare_notes: World-class private healthcare in Cape Town and Johannesburg. Private insurance essential. Good private hospitals comparable to US standards.

demographics.expat_worship_note: Christian churches of all denominations widely available. English-language services very common.

tax.summary: Progressive income tax 15-40%. No special retirement visa but long-term residence available. Istanbul popular with expats. High inflation environment in recent years.
tax.key_exemptions: No special pension exemptions. Foreign Tax Credit available. Dollar/euro purchasing power strong due to lira depreciation.
tax.top_reason: Affordable, Istanbul lifestyle, strong dollar value

visa.name: Short-Term Residence Permit
visa.summary: Short-term residence permit renewable annually. Low income requirements. Istanbul and coastal areas (Bodrum, Antalya) very popular with expats.
visa.healthcare_notes: Good private healthcare in major cities. International hospitals in Istanbul. Private insurance strongly recommended.

demographics.expat_worship_note: Mosques everywhere. Catholic, Protestant, and Orthodox churches in Istanbul and major cities.

tax.summary: Australia taxes residents on worldwide income at progressive rates up to 45% plus a 2% Medicare levy. US-Australia tax treaty in place but contains savings clause limiting benefits for US citizens. Foreign Tax Credit typically offsets most US tax liability. No special retirement tax regime.
tax.key_exemptions: US-Australia tax treaty helps reduce double taxation but does not eliminate US filing obligations. Foreign Tax Credit on Form 1116 is the primary relief tool. Temporary residents taxed only on Australian-source income. No special pension exemptions for US retirees.
tax.top_reason: English-speaking, high quality of life, excellent healthcare

visa.name: Subclass 405 / 410 (effectively closed) or Contributory Parent
visa.income_requirement_str: No standard retirement visa currently available
visa.summary: Australia's dedicated retirement visas (subclass 405 and 410) are effectively closed to new applicants. Most US retirees use partner visas, parent visas, or investor pathways. No straightforward retirement visa route currently exists. Consult an Australian immigration specialist.
visa.healthcare_notes: Excellent public Medicare system accessible to permanent residents. Private health insurance recommended and often required for visa. World-class hospitals in Sydney, Melbourne, and major cities. Medicare does not cover care in Australia.

expat.expat_vibe: Very easy transition for Americans. English, similar culture, outdoor lifestyle. Sydney and Melbourne world-class cities. High quality of life. Expensive but wages are also high. Limited retirement visa pathway is the main challenge.

demographics.expat_worship_note: Christian churches of all denominations widely available. English-language worship universally available.

tax.summary: Progressive income tax 20-45% plus National Insurance. US pensions taxable in UK as foreign pension income. UK state pension also taxable in US. US-UK tax treaty prevents double taxation via Foreign Tax Credit. Both countries tax pension income but credit offsets liability.
tax.key_exemptions: US-UK tax treaty (Article 17) governs pension taxation. Foreign Tax Credit on Form 1116 typically offsets most US liability. No special retirement exemption but treaty coordination reduces double tax.
tax.top_reason: English-speaking, culture, NHS healthcare

visa.name: Standard Visitor / Ancestry Visa
visa.summary: No dedicated retirement visa for non-EU nationals. UK Ancestry visa available if grandparent was UK citizen. Standard visitor visa allows 6-month stays. Long-term residency requires investment or family route.
visa.healthcare_notes: Excellent NHS public healthcare for legal residents. Private insurance recommended for faster access. World-class hospitals. Medicare does not cover UK care.

expat.expat_vibe: Most natural transition for Americans due to language and cultural similarities. London massive international city. Easy practical life. NHS healthcare highly regarded. No culture shock. Expensive but familiar.

demographics.expat_worship_note: All denominations available. English-language worship universally available.

direct_us_cities: All major US cities — most connected destination from US

tax.summary: Progressive income tax up to 40%. Most stable and developed economy in South America. No special retirement exemptions. US-Chile tax treaty in place.
tax.key_exemptions: US-Chile tax treaty helps reduce double taxation. Foreign Tax Credit available. Santiago has first-world infrastructure.
tax.top_reason: Most stable South American country, world-class Santiago

visa.name: Rentista Visa
visa.summary: Rentista visa for passive income earners. Santiago is the most developed city in South America. Diverse geography from desert to Patagonia.
visa.healthcare_notes: Excellent private healthcare in Santiago at affordable prices. Private insurance recommended. Best healthcare system in South America.

demographics.expat_worship_note: Catholic churches everywhere. English-language services in Santiago expat areas.

tax.summary: Tax rates vary significantly by canton. Federal rate up to 11.5% plus cantonal rates. Total effective rates typically 20-40%. US-Switzerland treaty in place. High cost of living but excellent quality of life. Lump-sum taxation available in some cantons for wealthy foreigners.
tax.key_exemptions: Lump-sum taxation (forfait fiscal) available for non-working foreigners in some cantons. US-Switzerland treaty reduces double taxation. Foreign Tax Credit available.
tax.top_reason: Safety, quality of life, beautiful scenery

visa.summary: Residence permit for financially independent persons. High wealth requirements. Zurich, Geneva, Basel popular but expensive. Excellent quality of life and safety.
visa.healthcare_notes: Excellent mandatory health insurance system. Private insurance required for all residents. Very high standard of care. Medicare not accepted.

tax.summary: Italy's Article 24-ter allows qualifying foreign pensioners to pay a flat 7% tax on all foreign income for up to 10 years. Must relocate to specific southern Italian municipalities. US-Italy tax treaty in place.
tax.key_exemptions: 7% flat tax on foreign pension income for up to 10 years. Must move to qualifying southern Italian town with population under 20,000. Significant savings vs standard progressive rates up to 43%.
tax.top_reason: 7% flat tax on foreign income for 10 years

visa.name: Elective Residency Visa
visa.summary: Elective Residency Visa for retirees with passive income. Requires proof of stable passive income and private health insurance. Leads to permanent residency after 5 years.
visa.healthcare_notes: Excellent public healthcare (SSN) accessible after registering as resident. Private insurance required for initial visa.

expat.expat_vibe: Strong American expat community especially in Florence and Rome. Many retirees drawn by 7% flat tax in southern villages. Italian bureaucracy notorious but expats navigate it. Warm culture once you're in.

demographics.expat_worship_note: Catholic churches everywhere. English-language Mass available in major cities. Protestant services in larger cities.

tax.summary: Romania has one of the lowest flat income tax rates in the EU at 10%. No special retirement regime but very low base rate. EU and Schengen member as of 2025. US-Romania tax treaty in place.
tax.key_exemptions: 10% flat tax on all income including pensions. Very favorable for US retirees as Foreign Tax Credit easily offsets US obligations. Bucharest emerging as expat hub.
tax.top_reason: 10% flat tax, very affordable EU country

visa.name: Long-term Residence Permit
visa.summary: Residence permit available for passive income earners. Low income requirements. EU and Schengen access as of 2025.
visa.healthcare_notes: Public healthcare available but quality is variable. Private insurance strongly recommended. Good private hospitals in Bucharest and major cities.

demographics.expat_worship_note: Orthodox churches everywhere. Catholic and Protestant services available. Limited English services.

tax.summary: Bulgaria has the lowest flat income tax in the EU at 10%. Applies to all income including foreign pensions. EU member. Very low cost of living. US-Bulgaria tax treaty in place.
tax.key_exemptions: 10% flat tax on worldwide income for residents. Foreign Tax Credit easily covers US obligations. No complex brackets or phase-outs.
tax.top_reason: 10% flat tax, lowest cost EU country

visa.name: Long-term Residence Permit
visa.summary: Long-term residence available for passive income earners. Low income threshold. EU Schengen access. Black Sea coast and mountain regions popular.
visa.healthcare_notes: Public healthcare available but quality is inconsistent. Private insurance strongly recommended. Good private clinics in Sofia.

demographics.expat_worship_note: Orthodox churches everywhere. Limited English-language services.

tax.summary: Progressive income tax 20-40% plus Universal Social Charge (USC) 0.5-8%. Foreign pensions taxable for residents. US-Ireland treaty in place. English-speaking EU member. Popular with US diaspora.
tax.key_exemptions: US-Ireland treaty reduces double taxation. Foreign Tax Credit available. Remittance basis available for non-domiciled residents. Large Irish-American community creates strong cultural ties.
tax.top_reason: English-speaking, EU member, strong US cultural ties

visa.name: Stamp 0 / Long Stay Visa
visa.summary: Stamp 0 for financially independent non-EU nationals. Requires proof of income and private health insurance. Dublin and rural Ireland popular. English speaking EU country.
visa.healthcare_notes: Public HSE healthcare for residents. Private insurance recommended for faster access. Good standard of care. Medicare not accepted.

tax.top_reason: Close to US, large expat communities, no local tax on foreign income

visa.name: Temporary Resident Visa
visa.summary: Temporary Resident Visa requires ~$2,700/month income or $108,000 in savings. Leads to permanent residency after 4 years. Large expat communities in San Miguel, Puerto Vallarta, Merida.
visa.healthcare_notes: Excellent private healthcare at fraction of US cost. IMSS public system available. Private insurance recommended. Many US expats use Mexican private care.

expat.expat_vibe: Largest American expat community in the world. Highly established infrastructure for expats. Easy transition for Americans. Some areas feel like American enclaves. Lake Chapala/Ajijic specifically known as top retirement community.

demographics.expat_worship_note: Catholic churches everywhere. English-language Protestant and non-denominational churches in major expat areas.

direct_us_cities: All major US cities — extensive direct routes

tax.summary: Progressive income tax 10-50%. New immigrants and returning residents receive 10-year tax exemption on foreign income. US-Israel treaty in place. Unique immigration law: Law of Return for Jewish immigrants.
tax.key_exemptions: 10-year foreign income tax exemption for new immigrants (Olim) and returning residents. US-Israel treaty reduces double taxation. Law of Return provides citizenship path for eligible Jewish immigrants.
tax.top_reason: 10-year foreign income exemption, Law of Return, world-class healthcare

visa.name: Aliyah (Law of Return) or temporary residency
visa.summary: Law of Return grants citizenship and residency rights to eligible Jewish immigrants and family members. Temporary residency available for others. Tel Aviv and Jerusalem major expat hubs.
visa.healthcare_notes: Excellent universal healthcare via health funds (kupot holim). Very high standard. English widely spoken in medical settings. Medicare not accepted.

tax.summary: Progressive income tax up to 49.5%. Foreign pensions taxable for residents. US-Netherlands treaty in place. 30% ruling available for some expats (not applicable to retirees). High tax but excellent quality of life.
tax.key_exemptions: US-Netherlands treaty reduces double taxation. Foreign Tax Credit available. No special retirement exemption. Amsterdam and Hague popular with international community.
tax.top_reason: English-speaking, liberal culture, central Europe

visa.name: MVV Long Stay Visa
visa.summary: MVV long-stay visa for financially independent persons. Leads to residence permit. Amsterdam, Hague, Rotterdam are major expat hubs. English widely spoken.
visa.healthcare_notes: Excellent mandatory health insurance. All residents must have basic health insurance. High standard of care. Medicare not accepted.

tax.summary: Progressive income tax 20-45% plus National Insurance. US pensions taxable in UK as foreign pension income. UK state pension also taxable in US. US-UK tax treaty prevents double taxation via Foreign Tax Credit. Both countries tax pension income but credit offsets liability.
tax.key_exemptions: US-UK tax treaty (Article 17) governs pension taxation. Foreign Tax Credit on Form 1116 typically offsets most US liability. No special retirement exemption but treaty coordination reduces double tax.
tax.top_reason: English-speaking, culture, NHS healthcare

visa.name: Standard Visitor / Ancestry Visa
visa.summary: No dedicated retirement visa for non-EU nationals. UK Ancestry visa available if grandparent was UK citizen. Standard visitor visa allows 6-month stays. Long-term residency requires investment or family route.
visa.healthcare_notes: Excellent NHS public healthcare for legal residents. Private insurance recommended for faster access. World-class hospitals. Medicare does not cover UK care.

expat.expat_vibe: Most natural transition for Americans due to language and cultural similarities. London massive international city. Easy practical life. NHS healthcare highly regarded. No culture shock. Expensive but familiar.

demographics.expat_worship_note: All denominations available. English-language worship universally available.

direct_us_cities: All major US cities — most connected destination from US

tax.summary: NHR regime closed to new applicants in 2024. Foreign pensions now taxed at standard progressive rates up to 48%. US-Portugal tax treaty helps reduce double taxation. Foreign Tax Credit usually offsets most US tax liability.
tax.key_exemptions: NHR 2.0 (IFICI) replaced NHR but excludes pension income. Existing NHR holders grandfathered through their 10-year period. US-Portugal tax treaty provides some relief.
tax.top_reason: Affordable Europe, English widely spoken

visa.name: D7 Passive Income Visa
visa.summary: D7 visa for passive income earners including retirees. Leads to permanent residency after 5 years, citizenship after 5 years. Requires proof of passive income and private health insurance.
visa.healthcare_notes: Public SNS healthcare accessible after residency validated. Private insurance required for visa application. High quality public system once enrolled.

expat.expat_vibe: One of the most welcoming expat destinations in Europe. Large English-speaking community. Many digital nomads and retirees. Portuguese people generally warm toward Americans.

demographics.expat_worship_note: Catholic churches everywhere. Protestant and international English-language services in Lisbon and Porto.

tax.summary: Progressive income tax 20-55%. High tax burden but excellent quality of life. US-Austria tax treaty in place. Foreign Tax Credit offsets US liability. Vienna consistently ranked world's most livable city.
tax.key_exemptions: US-Austria tax treaty reduces double taxation. Foreign Tax Credit available. No special retirement exemption. High taxes offset by excellent public services.
tax.top_reason: Vienna, world-class quality of life

visa.name: Residence Permit (Red-White-Red Card)
visa.summary: Residence permit for financially independent persons. Vienna is consistently the world's most livable city. Excellent public transport, culture, and healthcare.
visa.healthcare_notes: Excellent public healthcare for residents. Health insurance required. High standard of care. Medicare not accepted.

tax.summary: Italy's Article 24-ter allows qualifying foreign pensioners to pay a flat 7% tax on all foreign income for up to 10 years. Must relocate to specific southern Italian municipalities. US-Italy tax treaty in place.
tax.key_exemptions: 7% flat tax on foreign pension income for up to 10 years. Must move to qualifying southern Italian town with population under 20,000. Significant savings vs standard progressive rates up to 43%.
tax.top_reason: 7% flat tax on foreign income for 10 years

visa.name: Elective Residency Visa
visa.summary: Elective Residency Visa for retirees with passive income. Requires proof of stable passive income and private health insurance. Leads to permanent residency after 5 years.
visa.healthcare_notes: Excellent public healthcare (SSN) accessible after registering as resident. Private insurance required for initial visa.

expat.expat_vibe: Strong American expat community especially in Florence and Rome. Many retirees drawn by 7% flat tax in southern villages. Italian bureaucracy notorious but expats navigate it. Warm culture once you're in.

demographics.expat_worship_note: Catholic churches everywhere. English-language Mass available in major cities. Protestant services in larger cities.

tax.key_exemptions: US-India treaty reduces double taxation. Foreign Tax Credit available. Very low cost of living means modest income goes far. Special provisions for non-resident Indians (NRI) and returning expats.
