visa.summary: Residence permit for financially independent persons. High wealth requirements. Zurich, Geneva, Basel popular but expensive. Excellent quality of life and safety.
visa.healthcare_notes: Excellent mandatory health insurance system. Private insurance required for all residents. Very high standard of care. Medicare not accepted.

tax.rate_label: Foreign income remitted in same year taxable
tax.summary: Thailand taxes foreign income remitted to Thailand in the same calendar year as earned. Keep pension deposits in US accounts and transfer only what you need. New rules from 2024 tightened this. US-Thailand tax treaty in place.
tax.key_exemptions: Only remitted income taxable. Transfer savings from prior years tax-free. Keep funds in US accounts and withdraw as needed. Progressive rates 5-35% on taxable income.
tax.top_reason: World-class healthcare, affordable living, Buddhist culture

visa.name: Non-Immigrant O-A (Retirement)
visa.summary: O-A visa for age 50+. Requires health insurance with minimum $40,000 inpatient coverage. Annual renewal. 10-year O-X visa available for $85,000 deposit. Long-Term Resident visa for high earners.
visa.healthcare_notes: World-class private hospitals in Bangkok and Chiang Mai. JCI-accredited. International health insurance required for visa. Excellent care at fraction of US cost.

expat.expat_vibe: Massive global expat community. Chiang Mai is legendary for digital nomads and retirees. Bangkok world-class city at affordable prices. Extremely welcoming culture. Buddhist values create peaceful environment. Large retiree community especially in Hua Hin and Pattaya.

demographics.expat_worship_note: Buddhist temples everywhere. Churches in Bangkok and Chiang Mai. International English-language services available.

tax.summary: Progressive income tax with rates of 20% up to HRK 360,000 and 30% above. EU member since 2013, Eurozone since 2023. No special retirement tax regime. US-Croatia tax treaty in place.
tax.key_exemptions: No special pension exemption. Foreign Tax Credit offsets US tax liability. Relatively low tax rates compared to Western Europe.
tax.top_reason: Affordable EU country, Adriatic coastline

visa.name: Temporary Residence Permit
visa.summary: Third-country nationals (including US) can apply for temporary residence with proof of passive income. EU membership means strong rule of law and stability.
visa.healthcare_notes: Public healthcare available after residency registration. Quality is good in cities, limited in rural areas. Private insurance recommended.

demographics.expat_worship_note: Catholic churches everywhere. Limited non-Catholic options outside Zagreb.

tax.summary: Article 5B allows qualifying foreign pensioners to elect a 7% flat tax on all foreign source income for up to 15 years. Must come from a country with tax cooperation agreement with Greece. US qualifies.
tax.key_exemptions: 7% flat tax on all foreign income for up to 15 years. Requires minimum €250,000 real estate investment for Golden Visa OR €3,500/month income for retirement visa. Excellent tax deal for US retirees.
tax.top_reason: 7% flat tax on foreign income for 15 years

visa.name: Financially Independent Person Visa
visa.summary: Financially Independent Person Visa requires proof of passive income, health insurance, and clean criminal record. Golden Visa (real estate investment) also available. EU Schengen access.
visa.healthcare_notes: Public healthcare accessible after residency. Private insurance required for visa. High quality private hospitals in Athens and major cities.

expat.expat_vibe: Growing expat scene attracted by 7% flat tax program. Less established than Spain or Portugal but growing rapidly. Greek people warm and hospitable. Laid-back Mediterranean lifestyle.

demographics.expat_worship_note: Greek Orthodox churches everywhere. Catholic and Protestant churches in Athens and major tourist islands.

tax.summary: NHR regime closed to new applicants in 2024. Foreign pensions now taxed at standard progressive rates up to 48%. US-Portugal tax treaty helps reduce double taxation. Foreign Tax Credit usually offsets most US tax liability.
tax.key_exemptions: NHR 2.0 (IFICI) replaced NHR but excludes pension income. Existing NHR holders grandfathered through their 10-year period. US-Portugal tax treaty provides some relief.
tax.top_reason: Affordable Europe, English widely spoken

visa.name: D7 Passive Income Visa
visa.summary: D7 visa for passive income earners including retirees. Leads to permanent residency after 5 years, citizenship after 5 years. Requires proof of passive income and private health insurance.
visa.healthcare_notes: Public SNS healthcare accessible after residency validated. Private insurance required for visa application. High quality public system once enrolled.

expat.expat_vibe: One of the most welcoming expat destinations in Europe. Large English-speaking community. Many digital nomads and retirees. Portuguese people generally warm toward Americans.

demographics.expat_worship_note: Catholic churches everywhere. Protestant and international English-language services in Lisbon and Porto.

tax.summary: Progressive income tax 20-45% plus National Insurance. US pensions taxable in UK as foreign pension income. UK state pension also taxable in US. US-UK tax treaty prevents double taxation via Foreign Tax Credit. Both countries tax pension income but credit offsets liability.
tax.key_exemptions: US-UK tax treaty (Article 17) governs pension taxation. Foreign Tax Credit on Form 1116 typically offsets most US liability. No special retirement exemption but treaty coordination reduces double tax.
tax.top_reason: English-speaking, culture, NHS healthcare

visa.name: Standard Visitor / Ancestry Visa
visa.summary: No dedicated retirement visa for non-EU nationals. UK Ancestry visa available if grandparent was UK citizen. Standard visitor visa allows 6-month stays. Long-term residency requires investment or family route.
visa.healthcare_notes: Excellent NHS public healthcare for legal residents. Private insurance recommended for faster access. World-class hospitals. Medicare does not cover UK care.

expat.expat_vibe: Most natural transition for Americans due to language and cultural similarities. London massive international city. Easy practical life. NHS healthcare highly regarded. No culture shock. Expensive but familiar.

demographics.expat_worship_note: All denominations available. English-language worship universally available.

direct_us_cities: All major US cities — most connected destination from US

tax.summary: Article 5B allows qualifying foreign pensioners to elect a 7% flat tax on all foreign source income for up to 15 years. Must come from a country with tax cooperation agreement with Greece. US qualifies.
tax.key_exemptions: 7% flat tax on all foreign income for up to 15 years. Requires minimum €250,000 real estate investment for Golden Visa OR €3,500/month income for retirement visa. Excellent tax deal for US retirees.
tax.top_reason: 7% flat tax on foreign income for 15 years

visa.name: Financially Independent Person Visa
visa.summary: Financially Independent Person Visa requires proof of passive income, health insurance, and clean criminal record. Golden Visa (real estate investment) also available. EU Schengen access.
visa.healthcare_notes: Public healthcare accessible after residency. Private insurance required for visa. High quality private hospitals in Athens and major cities.

expat.expat_vibe: Growing expat scene attracted by 7% flat tax program. Less established than Spain or Portugal but growing rapidly. Greek people warm and hospitable. Laid-back Mediterranean lifestyle.

demographics.expat_worship_note: Greek Orthodox churches everywhere. Catholic and Protestant churches in Athens and major tourist islands.

tax.summary: Italy's Article 24-ter allows qualifying foreign pensioners to pay a flat 7% tax on all foreign income for up to 10 years. Must relocate to specific southern Italian municipalities. US-Italy tax treaty in place.
tax.key_exemptions: 7% flat tax on foreign pension income for up to 10 years. Must move to qualifying southern Italian town with population under 20,000. Significant savings vs standard progressive rates up to 43%.
tax.top_reason: 7% flat tax on foreign income for 10 years

visa.name: Elective Residency Visa
visa.summary: Elective Residency Visa for retirees with passive income. Requires proof of stable passive income and private health insurance. Leads to permanent residency after 5 years.
visa.healthcare_notes: Excellent public healthcare (SSN) accessible after registering as resident. Private insurance required for initial visa.

expat.expat_vibe: Strong American expat community especially in Florence and Rome. Many retirees drawn by 7% flat tax in southern villages. Italian bureaucracy notorious but expats navigate it. Warm culture once you're in.

demographics.expat_worship_note: Catholic churches everywhere. English-language Mass available in major cities. Protestant services in larger cities.

tax.summary: Progressive income tax 14-45% plus 5.5% solidarity surcharge. Foreign pensions taxable for German residents. US-Germany tax treaty coordinates taxation. Foreign Tax Credit handles most double taxation. High quality of life but significant tax burden.
tax.key_exemptions: US-Germany tax treaty provides pension tax coordination. Foreign Tax Credit offsets US liability. No special retirement exemption. High cost but excellent infrastructure and healthcare.
tax.top_reason: World-class infrastructure, central Europe location

visa.name: Residence Permit for Financial Independence
visa.summary: Residence permit available for financially independent persons. Requires proof of sufficient income and health insurance. EU Schengen access. Berlin, Munich, Hamburg popular with expats.
visa.healthcare_notes: World-class public healthcare system. Health insurance mandatory for all residents. Private insurance available. Excellent standard of care. Medicare not accepted.

expat.expat_vibe: Berlin especially has massive international expat scene. Very liberal and creative city. English widely spoken. German efficiency makes practical life easy. Good public transport. Strong social services.

demographics.expat_worship_note: Catholic and Protestant churches everywhere. International English-language services in major cities.

tax.summary: Italy's Article 24-ter allows qualifying foreign pensioners to pay a flat 7% tax on all foreign income for up to 10 years. Must relocate to specific southern Italian municipalities. US-Italy tax treaty in place.
tax.key_exemptions: 7% flat tax on foreign pension income for up to 10 years. Must move to qualifying southern Italian town with population under 20,000. Significant savings vs standard progressive rates up to 43%.
tax.top_reason: 7% flat tax on foreign income for 10 years

visa.name: Elective Residency Visa
visa.summary: Elective Residency Visa for retirees with passive income. Requires proof of stable passive income and private health insurance. Leads to permanent residency after 5 years.
visa.healthcare_notes: Excellent public healthcare (SSN) accessible after registering as resident. Private insurance required for initial visa.

expat.expat_vibe: Strong American expat community especially in Florence and Rome. Many retirees drawn by 7% flat tax in southern villages. Italian bureaucracy notorious but expats navigate it. Warm culture once you're in.

demographics.expat_worship_note: Catholic churches everywhere. English-language Mass available in major cities. Protestant services in larger cities.

tax.summary: Canada taxes residents on worldwide income. US-Canada tax treaty coordinates taxation of US pensions and Social Security. Foreign Tax Credit typically offsets most double taxation. Provincial taxes add 5-25% on top of federal rates.
tax.key_exemptions: US-Canada tax treaty is one of the most comprehensive. Social Security benefits may be partially exempt. Foreign Tax Credit handles most double taxation. No special retirement visa but immigration pathways available.
tax.top_reason: English-speaking, safe, familiar culture

visa.name: Super Visa / Express Entry
visa.income_requirement_str: Super Visa: child/grandchild must meet income threshold
visa.summary: No dedicated retirement visa. Super Visa allows parents/grandparents of Canadian citizens to stay up to 5 years. Express Entry for those with qualifying skills or family. Popular for those with Canadian family connections.
visa.healthcare_notes: Excellent universal healthcare for legal residents. Provincial health coverage varies. Private insurance recommended while establishing residency. Medicare does not cover Canadian care.

expat.expat_vibe: Most natural transition from US. Familiar culture, English language, similar systems. Victoria BC particularly popular with US retirees for mild climate. Montreal bilingual and culturally distinct. Toronto world-class diverse city.

demographics.expat_worship_note: All denominations widely available. English-language worship universally available.

direct_us_cities: All major US cities — extensive cross-border routes

tax.key_exemptions: US-Canada tax treaty is one of the most comprehensive. Social Security benefits may be partially exempt. Foreign Tax Credit handles most double taxation. No special retirement visa but immigration pathways available.
tax.top_reason: English-speaking, safe, familiar culture

visa.name: Super Visa / Express Entry
visa.income_requirement_str: Super Visa: child/grandchild must meet income threshold
visa.summary: No dedicated retirement visa. Super Visa allows parents/grandparents of Canadian citizens to stay up to 5 years. Express Entry for those with qualifying skills or family. Popular for those with Canadian family connections.
visa.healthcare_notes: Excellent universal healthcare for legal residents. Provincial health coverage varies. Private insurance recommended while establishing residency. Medicare does not cover Canadian care.

expat.expat_vibe: Most natural transition from US. Familiar culture, English language, similar systems. Victoria BC particularly popular with US retirees for mild climate. Montreal bilingual and culturally distinct. Toronto world-class diverse city.

demographics.expat_worship_note: All denominations widely available. English-language worship universally available.

direct_us_cities: All major US cities — extensive cross-border routes

tax.summary: Progressive income tax up to 47.4%. Foreign pensions taxable for residents. US-Norway treaty in place. Very high cost of living but exceptional quality of life. Oil fund wealth supports excellent public services.
tax.key_exemptions: US-Norway treaty reduces double taxation. Foreign Tax Credit available. No special retirement exemption. High cost offset by strong purchasing power.
tax.top_reason: Natural beauty, safety, exceptional quality of life

visa.name: Residence Permit
visa.summary: Residence permit for financially independent persons. Oslo is very expensive. Beautiful fjord country. Very high standard of living.
visa.healthcare_notes: Excellent universal healthcare. Very high standard. Medicare not accepted.

tax.summary: NHR regime closed to new applicants in 2024. Foreign pensions now taxed at standard progressive rates up to 48%. US-Portugal tax treaty helps reduce double taxation. Foreign Tax Credit usually offsets most US tax liability.
tax.key_exemptions: NHR 2.0 (IFICI) replaced NHR but excludes pension income. Existing NHR holders grandfathered through their 10-year period. US-Portugal tax treaty provides some relief.
tax.top_reason: Affordable Europe, English widely spoken

visa.name: D7 Passive Income Visa
visa.summary: D7 visa for passive income earners including retirees. Leads to permanent residency after 5 years, citizenship after 5 years. Requires proof of passive income and private health insurance.
visa.healthcare_notes: Public SNS healthcare accessible after residency validated. Private insurance required for visa application. High quality public system once enrolled.

expat.expat_vibe: One of the most welcoming expat destinations in Europe. Large English-speaking community. Many digital nomads and retirees. Portuguese people generally warm toward Americans.

demographics.expat_worship_note: Catholic churches everywhere. Protestant and international English-language services in Lisbon and Porto.

tax.summary: Progressive income tax up to 27.5%. Foreign pensions may be taxable depending on residency status. Complex tax system. US-Brazil tax treaty limited. Consult tax professional.
tax.key_exemptions: Some foreign pension exemptions available. Foreign Tax Credit for US obligations. Tax rules complex and frequently change.
tax.top_reason: Diverse lifestyle options, beaches, vibrant culture

visa.name: Retirement Visa (VITEM XIV)
visa.summary: Retirement visa for those with provable pension income. Large country with diverse options from beaches to cities. Large expat communities in Florianopolis and Sao Paulo.
visa.healthcare_notes: Good private healthcare in major cities. SUS public system available but variable quality. Private insurance strongly recommended.

demographics.expat_worship_note: Catholic and Evangelical churches everywhere. English-language services in major cities.
